mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration and elevated recession probability, causing institutional investors to de-risk portfolios and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear market outcomes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real retail sales and housing starts confirm synchronized consumer spending and residential investment strength, validating robust earnings growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in more than 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rebound in the index of industrial production following a prior decline signals a supply-side recovery that resolves inventory constraints and improves corporate margin expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are negative in more than 60% of rebound instances, or the signal loses predictive power during high volatility regimes with VIX above 25.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion that directly supports corporate revenue growth and dividend payouts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where GDP growth exceeds the 2.0 percent threshold, or the signal shows zero incremental value over a risk-free rate benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and higher borrowing costs, compressing equity discount factors and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 60% of high-inflation months due to growth-outweighing-rate dynamics, or the threshold fails to predict direction in low-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm healthy labor market expansion that strengthens consumer spending power and corporate earnings forecasts, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences over a 4-year rolling window, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from independent single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail sales exceeding 1.0 percent signals immediate consumer demand contraction that forces rapid earnings downgrades and triggers algorithmic portfolio rebalancing, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction during low-volatility regimes where mean reversion dominates.
